info.title: RunwayML API
info.description: The API makes generative AI models available, at the same credits prices listed here ($0.01 per credit): https://help.runwayml.com/hc/en-us/articles/15124877443219-How-do-credits-work

get.description: Return details about a task. Consumers of this API should not expect updates more frequent than once every five seconds for a given task.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

task_id = '17f20503-6c24-4c16-946b-35dbbce2af2f'

# Fetch the current state of the task:
task = client.tasks.retrieve(id=task_id)
print(task)

# Or, wait for the task to succeed or fail:
task = client.tasks.retrieve(id=task_id).wait_for_task_output()
print(task)

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of a previously-submitted task that has not been canceled or deleted.

id.description: The ID of the task being returned.

createdAt.description: The timestamp that the task was submitted at.

id.description: The ID of the task being returned.

createdAt.description: The timestamp that the task was submitted at.

id.description: The ID of the task being returned.

createdAt.description: The timestamp that the task was submitted at.

id.description: The ID of the task being returned.

createdAt.description: The timestamp that the task was submitted at.

id.description: The ID of the task being returned.

createdAt.description: The timestamp that the task was submitted at.

failure.description: A human-friendly reason for the failure. We do not recommend returning this to users directly without adding context.

failureCode.description: A machine-readable error code for the failure. See https://docs.dev.runwayml.com/errors/task-failures/ for more information.

id.description: The ID of the task being returned.

createdAt.description: The timestamp that the task was submitted at.

output.description: An array of URLs that return the output of the task. These URLs will expire within 24-48 hours; fetch the task again to get fresh URLs. It is expected that you download the assets at these URLs and store them in your own storage system.

pending.title: Pending task
pending.summary: An example of a pending task

throttled.title: Throttled task
throttled.summary: An example of a throttled task

running.title: Running task
running.summary: An example of a running task

succeeded.title: Succeeded task
succeeded.summary: An example of a succeeded task

failed.title: Failed task
failed.summary: An example of a failed task

value.failure: The provided image was flagged by content moderation.

404.description: The task does not exist, or was deleted or canceled.

delete.description:
Tasks that are running, pending, or throttled can be canceled by invoking this method. Invoking this method for other tasks will delete them.

The output data associated with a deleted task will be deleted from persistent storage in accordance with our data retention policy. Aborted and deleted tasks will not be able to be fetched again in the future.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

client.tasks.delete(id='17f20503-6c24-4c16-946b-35dbbce2af2f')

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of a previously-submitted task that has not been canceled or deleted.

204.description: This endpoint will produce a 204 No Content response indicating successful cancellation or deletion. Calling this endpoint for tasks which have already been deleted or aborted will result in a 404: for idempotency purposes, it is safe to ignore these 404 errors.

post.description: This endpoint will start a new task to generate a video from an image.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

task = client.image_to_video.create(
  model='gen4_turbo',
  prompt_image='https://example.com/bunny.jpg',
  prompt_text='A cute bunny hopping in a meadow',
  duration=10,
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

promptText.description: A non-empty string up to 1000 characters (measured in UTF-16 code units). This should describe in detail what should appear in the output.

anyOf.description: A HTTPS URL, Runway or data URI containing an encoded image. See [our docs](/assets/inputs#images) on image inputs for more information.
A HTTPS URL.
A Runway upload URI. See https://docs.dev.runwayml.com/assets/uploads for more information.
A data URI containing encoded media.

anyOf.title: PromptImages
anyOf.description: An array of objects representing images to be used in the output video. Only a `first` frame is supported.

uri.description: A HTTPS URL, Runway or data URI containing an encoded image. See [our docs](/assets/inputs#images) on image inputs for more information.

anyOf.description: A HTTPS URL.
A Runway upload URI. See https://docs.dev.runwayml.com/assets/uploads for more information.
A data URI containing encoded media.

position.description: The position of the image in the output video. "first" will use the image as the first frame of the video.

seed.description: If unspecified, a random number is chosen. Varying the seed integer is a way to get different results for the same other request parameters. Using the same seed integer for an identical request will produce similar results.

ratio.description: The resolution of the output video.

duration.description: The number of seconds of duration for the output video.

# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

task = client.text_to_video.create(
  model='veo3.1',
  prompt_text='A cute bunny hopping in a meadow',
  ratio='1280:720',
  duration=8,
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

task = client.video_to_video.create(
  model='gen4_aleph',
  video_uri='https://example.com/bunny.mp4',
  prompt_text='string',
  references=[
    {
      'type': 'image',
      'uri': 'https://example.com/easter-scene.jpg',
    },
  ],
  ratio='1280:720',
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

  model='gen4_image',

  ratio='1360:768',
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

post.description: This endpoint will start a new task to control a character's facial expressions and body movements using a reference video.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

task = client.character_performance.create(
  model='act_two',
  character={
    'type': 'video',
    'uri': 'https://example.com/posedCharacter.mp4',
  },
  reference={
    'type': 'video',
    'uri': 'https://example.com/actorPerformance.mp4',
  },
  ratio='1280:720',
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

seed.description: If unspecified, a random number is chosen. Varying the seed integer is a way to get different results for the same other request parameters. Using the same seed integer for an identical request will produce similar results.

character.description: The character to control. You can either provide a video or an image. A visually recognizable face must be visible and stay within the frame.

oneOf.title: CharacterImage
oneOf.description: An image of your character. In the output, the character will use the reference video performance in its original static environment.

uri.description: A HTTPS URL, Runway or data URI containing an encoded image. See [our docs](/assets/inputs#images) on image inputs for more information.

anyOf.description: A HTTPS URL.
A Runway upload URI. See https://docs.dev.runwayml.com/assets/uploads for more information.
A data URI containing encoded media.

oneOf.title: CharacterVideo
oneOf.description: A video of your character. In the output, the character will use the reference video performance in its original animated environment and some of the character's own movements.

uri.description: A HTTPS URL, Runway or data URI containing an encoded video. See [our docs](/assets/inputs#videos) on video inputs for more information.

anyOf.description: A HTTPS URL.
A Runway upload URI. See https://docs.dev.runwayml.com/assets/uploads for more information.
A data URI containing encoded media.

reference.description: The reference video containing the performance to apply to the character.

oneOf.title: CharacterReferenceVideo
oneOf.description: A video of a person performing in the manner that you would like your character to perform. The video must be between 3 and 30 seconds in duration.

uri.description: A video of a person performing in the manner that you would like your character to perform. The video must be between 3 and 30 seconds in duration. See [our docs](/assets/inputs#videos) on video inputs for more information.

anyOf.description: A HTTPS URL.
A Runway upload URI. See https://docs.dev.runwayml.com/assets/uploads for more information.
A data URI containing encoded media.

bodyControl.description: A boolean indicating whether to enable body control. When enabled, non-facial movements and gestures will be applied to the character in addition to facial expressions.

expressionIntensity.description: An integer between 1 and 5 (inclusive). A larger value increases the intensity of the character's expression.

ratio.description: The resolution of the output video.

contentModeration.description: Settings that affect the behavior of the content moderation system.

publicFigureThreshold.description: When set to `low`, the content moderation system will be less strict about preventing generations that include recognizable public figures.

id.description: The ID of the task that was created. Use this to retrieve the task later.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit for this endpoint.

post.description: This endpoint will start a new task to generate sound effects from a text description.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

task = client.sound_effect.create(
  model='eleven_text_to_sound_v2',
  promptText='A thunderstorm with heavy rain',
  duration=10,
  loop=True,
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

promptText.description: A text description of the sound effect to generate.

duration.description: The duration of the sound effect in seconds, between 0.5 and 30 seconds. If not provided, the duration will be determined automatically based on the text description.

loop.description: Whether the output sound effect should be designed to loop seamlessly.

id.description: The ID of the task that was created. Use this to retrieve the task later.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit for this endpoint.

post.description: This endpoint will start a new task to convert speech from one voice to another in audio or video.

voice.description: The voice to use for the generated speech.

id.description: The ID of the task that was created. Use this to retrieve the task later.

Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

task = client.text_to_speech.create(
  model='eleven_multilingual_v2',
  prompt_text='The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog',
  voice={
    'type': 'runway-preset',
    'presetId': 'Leslie',
  },
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

promptText.description: A non-empty string up to 1000 characters (measured in UTF-16 code units). This should describe in detail what should appear in the output.

voice.description: The voice to use for the generated speech.

oneOf.title: RunwayPresetVoice
oneOf.description: A voice preset from the RunwayML API.

presetId.description: The preset voice ID to use for the generated speech.

id.description: The ID of the task that was created. Use this to retrieve the task later.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit for this endpoint.

post.description: This endpoint will start a new task to dub audio content to a target language.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

task = client.voice_dubbing.create(
  model='eleven_voice_dubbing',
  audio_uri='https://example.com/audio.mp3',
  target_lang='es',
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

audioUri.description: A HTTPS URL, Runway or data URI containing an encoded audio. See [our docs](/assets/inputs#audio) on audio inputs for more information.

anyOf.description: A HTTPS URL.
A Runway upload URI. See https://docs.dev.runwayml.com/assets/uploads for more information.
A data URI containing encoded media.

targetLang.description: The target language code to dub the audio to (e.g., "es" for Spanish, "fr" for French).

disableVoiceCloning.description: Whether to disable voice cloning and use a generic voice instead.

dropBackgroundAudio.description: Whether to remove background audio from the dubbed output.

numSpeakers.description: The number of speakers in the audio. If not provided, it will be detected automatically.

id.description: The ID of the task that was created. Use this to retrieve the task later.

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit for this endpoint.

post.description: This endpoint will start a new task to isolate the voice from the background audio. Audio duration must be greater than 4.6 seconds and less than 3600 seconds.

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.

task = client.voice_isolation.create(

get.description: Get usage tier and credit balance information about the organization associated with the API key used to make the request.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

details = client.organization.retrieve()
print(details.creditBalance)

tier.title: OrganizationTierDetails
tier.description: Limits associated with the organization's tier.

maxMonthlyCreditSpend.description: The maximum number of credits that can be purchased in a month.

models.description: An object containing model-specific limits. Each key represents a model.

maxConcurrentGenerations.description: The maximum number of generations that can be run concurrently for this model.

maxDailyGenerations.description: The maximum number of generations that can be created each day for this model.

creditBalance.description: The number of credits remaining in the organization account.

dailyGenerations.description: The number of generations that have been run for this model in the past day.

post.description: Fetch credit usage data broken down by model and day for the organization associated with the API key used to make the request. Up to 90 days of data can be queried at a time.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

usage = client.organization.retrieve_usage()
print(usage)

startDate.description: The start date of the usage data in ISO-8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). If unspecified, it will default to 30 days before the current date. All dates are in UTC.

beforeDate.description: The end date of the usage data in ISO-8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD), not inclusive. If unspecified, it will default to thirty days after the start date. Must be less than or equal to 90 days after the start date. All dates are in UTC.

date.description: The date of the usage data in ISO-8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). All dates are in UTC.

usedCredits.description: The credits used per model for the given date.

model.title: ModelVariant
model.description: The model that credits were spent on.

amount.description: The number of credits spent on the model.

models.description: The list of models with usage during the queried time range.

items.title: ModelVariant
items.description: A model that was used during the queried time range.

post.description: Uploads a temporary media file that can be referenced in API generation requests. The uploaded files will be automatically expired and deleted after a period of time. It is strongly recommended to use our SDKs for this which have a simplified interface that directly accepts file objects.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML
from pathlib import Path

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

file = Path('./funny-cats.mp4');
upload_uri = client.uploads.create_ephemeral(
  file=file,
)

# Use the upload_uri in generation requests
task = client.video_to_video.create(
  model='gen4_aleph',
  video_uri=upload_uri,
  prompt_text='Add the easter elements to the cat video',
  references=[
    {
      'type': 'image',
      'uri': 'https://example.com/easter-scene.jpg',
    },
  ],
  ratio='1280:720',
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

filename.description: The filename of the file to upload. Must have a valid extension and be a supported media type (image, video, or audio).

type.description: The type of upload to create

200.description: The upload URL and Runway URI have been successfully created.

uploadUrl.description: The URL to upload your media file to with a POST request.

fields.description: Fields that must be included in the file upload request as form data.

runwayUri.description: The Runway upload URI to use in other API generation requests

429.description: You have exceeded the rate limit for this endpoint.

X-Runway-Version.name: X-Runway-Version
X-Runway-Version.description: The version of the RunwayML API being used. You can read more about versioning [here](/api-details/versioning).

tags.name: Start generating
tags.description: These endpoints all kick off tasks to create generations.

tags.name: Task management
tags.description: Endpoints for managing tasks that have been submitted.
